regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect southeast winds at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 36% rain probability, so drizzle may affect embarkation and shore excursions. Port Everglades will see similar conditions with east-southeast winds near 9 knots and a 36% chance of drizzle that could impact operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Miami and Port Everglades will experience increasing drizzle with east-southeast winds strengthening to 10 knots and a 62% rain probability, which is expected to impact port activities and outdoor excursions. By day three, drizzle will continue in Miami with winds around 11 knots and a 49% rain probability, so light rain could affect operations but with slightly less intensity. Keep an eye on these steady east-southeast winds and persistent drizzle as they may require adjustments to tender schedules and outdoor plans at these ports.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, expect easterly-southeast winds at 13 knots with drizzle and a 39% rain probability, which may affect embarkation and shore excursions in Nassau and nearby Freeport. Winds will increase to 17 knots from the same direction, sustaining drizzle and a 39% rain probability, potentially impacting port operations in Bimini and the Berry Islands. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly-southeast winds will strengthen to 18 knots with drizzle continuing and rain probabilities near 35%, conditions that could affect cruise activity in Key West and Grand Cayman. By day three, the wind remains steady at 18 knots ESE with drizzle and a 30% rain probability, possibly influencing operations in Ocho Rios, Falmouth, and Puerto Plata. Passengers should remain prepared for light rain and moderate winds through this period as vessels adjust docking and tendering accordingly.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, winds are east-southeast at 15 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 20% rain probability in San Juan, resulting in generally smooth embarkation and disembarkation conditions with minimal impact on shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will strengthen to 19 knots with drizzle and a 52% rain probability, which may affect port operations and shore excursions particularly in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten due to increased moisture and reduced visibility. By Day 3, winds remain steady at 18 knots from the east with drizzle and a 28% rain probability, suggesting a decrease in rain impact but continued damp conditions that could mildly influence outdoor activities in St. Croix and Antigua. Cruise vessels should prepare for persistent east winds between 15 and 19 knots over this period, with drizzle conditions moderating visibility and requiring cautious maneuvering during docking and tendering operations. Passengers should plan accordingly for possible wet conditions and variable visibility when planning shore excursions during these days.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing southeast winds at 6 knots with clear skies and a 20% rain probability, allowing for smooth port operations and shore excursions with minimal weather concerns. Beyond 48 hours, the trend toward increased rain probability and continued light easterly winds suggests maintaining readiness for localized showers, particularly in Cozumel, while overall marine conditions remain moderate.
